channel.title: Load-Balancer on Pi Stack
channel.description: Recent content in Load-Balancer on Pi Stack

item.title: MetalLB vs kube-vip vs Cilium LB: Bare-Metal Kubernetes Load Balancer Guide 2026
item.description: Running Kubernetes on bare metal or self-hosted infrastructure means you don’t have a cloud provider’s native load balancer. When you deploy a Service of type LoadBalancer, it stays in Pending state forever unless you install a load balancer controller. This guide compares the three leading open-source solutions: MetalLB, kube-vip, and Cilium LoadBalancer.

item.title: Self-Hosted HAProxy Management: Data Plane API vs Prometheus Exporter vs Runtime API 2026
item.description: HAProxy powers millions of production deployments as the world’s most widely-used open-source load balancer and reverse proxy. But managing it at scale — adding backends, checking stats, rotating SSL certs, and monitoring health — requires more than editing a static haproxy.cfg file and sending SIGHUP.

item.title: Keepalived vs Corosync + Pacemaker: Self-Hosted High Availability Clustering Guide 2026
item.description: When a single server handles all traffic for a critical service, one hardware failure, kernel panic, or network partition takes your entire application offline. High availability (HA) clustering eliminates this single point of failure by running services across multiple nodes that automatically detect failures and redirect traffic to healthy members.
